data.title: Domo COO Resigns and Transitions to Consulting Role
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Domo ( (DOMO) ) has provided an update.

On January 11, 2026, Domo, Inc. entered into a separation and settlement agreement with its Chief Operating Officer, Mark Maughan, under which he resigned from his role effective immediately and transitioned to a consulting position under a separate consulting agreement dated the same day. Under the separation terms, Maughan is to receive a $1.5 million cash payment within 30 days of the effective date, full accelerated vesting of his outstanding restricted stock units, and an additional 245,000 fully vested RSUs over three years, with any unpaid amounts accelerating upon a change in control; both parties also agreed to mutual releases of claims and Maughan committed to a non-compete during his consulting period, highlighting a structured leadership change with defined economic and legal protections for both sides.

The most recent analyst rating on (DOMO) stock is a Buy with a $22.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Domo stock, see the DOMO Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DOMO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DOMO is a Neutral.

Domo’s overall stock score is primarily impacted by its weak financial performance and valuation challenges. However, the positive sentiment from the earnings call, highlighting strategic initiatives and improvements in cash flow and operating margin, provides some optimism. Technical analysis indicates bearish momentum, adding to the cautious outlook.

To see Spark’s full report on DOMO stock, click here.

data.title: Pelican Advances Greenland-Focused Energy Business Combination Plan
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Pelican Acquisition Corporation ( (PELI) ) has provided an announcement.

In early January 2026, Pelican Acquisition Corporation highlighted recent Barron’s and Yahoo Finance coverage examining Greenland’s strategic Arctic position, its substantial but technically challenging rare earth and oil resources, and the broader geopolitical stakes surrounding U.S. interest in the Danish territory. The company also outlined progress on its planned multi-party business combination with March GL, Greenland Exploration Limited and Pelican Holdco, Inc., noting that a registration statement on Form S-4 has been filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission and that Pelican shareholders will be asked to vote on the transaction, which, if completed, would create a publicly listed Greenland-focused energy company with direct exposure to the island’s resource development amid significant regulatory, operational and market risks.

More about Pelican Acquisition Corporation

Pelican Acquisition Corporation is a special purpose acquisition company pursuing a business combination in the energy and natural resources sector, specifically focused on Greenland’s hydrocarbon and mineral potential through a planned merger with March GL, Greenland Exploration Limited and Pelican Holdco, Inc. into a go-forward public entity to be known as Greenland Energy Company.

Average Trading Volume: 40,528

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $123.7M

Learn more about PELI stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Momentus Revises Convertible Note Terms, Boosting Dilution Risk
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Momentus ( (MNTS) ) has provided an announcement.

On June 30, 2025, Momentus Inc. issued an unsecured convertible promissory note for $500,000 to A.G.P./Alliance Global Partners, which after a 1-for-17.85 reverse stock split on December 17, 2025, became convertible into Class A common shares at a price of $29.8095 per share. On January 9, 2026, the company exchanged this original note for a new unsecured convertible promissory note to A.G.P. on substantially the same terms and principal amount but with a reduced conversion price of $10.00 per share, a change that increases the potential equity dilution for existing shareholders while potentially making conversion more attractive to the noteholder; the exchange was conducted under a registration exemption pursuant to the Securities Act of 1933.

The most recent analyst rating on (MNTS) stock is a Sell with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Momentus stock, see the MNTS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MNTS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MNTS is a Neutral.

Momentus’s overall stock score is primarily impacted by its poor financial performance, characterized by declining revenues, high operating losses, and a weak balance sheet. Technical analysis also indicates bearish momentum, further contributing to the low score. The negative P/E ratio and lack of dividend yield result in a poor valuation score. These factors collectively highlight significant risks for investors.

To see Spark’s full report on MNTS stock, click here.

More about Momentus

Momentus Inc. is a Delaware-incorporated company that issues Class A common stock and uses convertible promissory notes as a financing tool, reflecting an equity-linked capital structure focused on funding its operations through securities offerings.

Vale SA ( (VALE) ) has provided an announcement.

On January 12, 2026, Vale S.A. disclosed that Capital World Investors had increased its stake in the miner to 227,690,911 common shares, equivalent to 5.02% of Vale’s total issued share capital, up from 4.61% previously. The investment, along with additional minority holdings in Vale shares by other Capital Research and Management Company divisions, remains non-controlling and does not alter Vale’s ownership or management structure, but underscores continued institutional investor confidence in the company without signaling any immediate change in its strategic direction or governance.

In the last 24 hours, Quantum Computing Inc. (QUBT) has been in focus after announcing it is the stalking horse bidder to acquire selected remaining assets of bankrupt Luminar Technologies for about $22 million in cash, complementing its previously announced purchase of Luminar Semiconductor. Analysts view these deals as strategically important because they would extend QCi’s presence across the photonics value chain—from chip design and packaging through full LiDAR system deployment—potentially adding immediate revenue, manufacturing scale, and specialized engineering talent while strengthening its position in quantum sensing, computing, communications, and AI-related markets. At the same time, commentary around the stock emphasizes execution and integration risks, the company’s current lack of meaningful, consistent revenues, and ongoing unprofitability and negative cash flows, which temper enthusiasm despite the large long-term market opportunity in quantum technologies. Recent research coverage and AI-based assessments reflect this balance: they cite QCi’s technical strength in photonics-based quantum networking and sensing and the growth potential of the industry, but also highlight competitive pressures from better-funded rivals and the uncertainty around bankruptcy court approvals and auction outcomes for the Luminar assets. These mixed fundamentals and risk factors, rather than any single earnings event, underpin the latest analyst views and implied price expectations for QUBT over the near term.

data.title: Wave of Insider Moves Rocks Phreesia’s Executive Suite
data.description:
New insider activity at Phreesia ( (PHR) ) has taken place on January 12, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Several top executives at Phreesia have recently sold significant amounts of company stock. GC & Secretary Allison Hoffman sold 6,585 shares for $111,945, while CEO Chaim Indig sold 8,440 shares for $143,480. President, Provider Solutions Evan Roberts sold 6,317 shares for $107,389, and President, Network Solutions David Linetsky sold 6,106 shares for $103,802. SVP HR Amy Beth VanDuyn sold 2,602 shares for $44,234. CFO Balaji Gandhi completed one of the larger transactions, selling 8,009 shares for $136,153. These insider sales collectively highlight a notable wave of stock activity among Phreesia’s leadership team.

Recent Updates on PHR stock

In the last 24 hours, Phreesia has seen a cluster of analyst actions driven more by evolving views of its growth and profitability profile than by any single new data point. RBC Capital resumed coverage, citing the company’s pivot toward profitability and the potential to accelerate growth and expand margins by more fully monetizing its patient-intake network across thousands of physicians and broader healthcare utilization. Wells Fargo initiated coverage emphasizing Phreesia’s ownership of key workflows (intake, scheduling, payments) for a sizable share of U.S. patient visits and arguing that its revenue growth since IPO is not reflected in the current valuation, which they no longer see as a major overhang. In contrast, Citi trimmed its target as part of a sector-wide reset after another difficult year for health tech stocks, pointing to broader market headwinds even as it remains cautiously optimistic into 2026. Truist also reduced its target, noting that the stock underperformed in 2025 despite management’s deliberate shift from pure growth to earnings strength and highlighting persistent investor skepticism about the durability of revenue growth; it now views Phreesia as needing multiple quarters of revenue and EBITDA outperformance to restore confidence. Barclays lowered its target after updating its model for Q3 results and the closing of the OneAccess deal but characterized the recent share-price selloff as surprising, implying that the fundamentals and strategic moves do not fully explain the magnitude of the market reaction. Overall, the news flow reflects a tension between improving profitability and platform strength on one hand and macro health-tech weakness and growth skepticism on the other, which has contributed to recent volatility in Phreesia’s share price.

Spark’s Take on PHR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PHR is a Neutral.

Phreesia’s overall stock score is driven by strong financial performance and positive earnings call highlights, including revenue growth and strategic acquisitions. Over the last 24 hours, PTC Therapeutics’ stock action has been driven mainly by stronger-than-expected preliminary 2025 results and a supportive shift in analyst sentiment. The company pre-announced about $823.4 million in 2025 product and royalty revenue, coming in above prior guidance, with particularly strong momentum from the newly launched Sephience, which delivered robust fourth-quarter sales and rapid patient uptake. In addition, PTC reported ending 2025 with roughly $1.94 billion in cash and securities, helped by fully monetizing its Evrysdi royalty interest via a sale to Royalty Pharma, which boosts near-term liquidity but removes future royalty income. The firm also highlighted regulatory and pipeline progress, including FDA alignment on a Phase 3 Huntington’s trial for votoplam and continued engagement on vatiquinone and Translarna, while 2026 guidance signaled ongoing heavy R&D and SG&A investment as it works toward cash-flow breakeven. Against this backdrop, Morgan Stanley raised its price target on PTCT, citing a favorable outlook for small- and mid-cap commercial biotechs expected to transition from being capital consumers to capital producers, and viewing PTC’s commercial trajectory and cash position as well-positioned relative to large biopharma peers facing patent cliffs, even as balance-sheet leverage and technical factors remain watch points. Recent AI-driven assessments also emphasize strong operational momentum and an undervalued earnings profile as positives, tempered by leverage and free-cash-flow risks.

Spark’s Take on PTCT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PTCT is a Neutral.

The score is driven by strong operational momentum (high TTM revenue growth, strong margins, and positive earnings-call guidance around Sephience) and an inexpensive P/E. Offsetting factors are balance-sheet risk from negative equity/high leverage and only neutral technical momentum.

At the same time, a newly announced $28M+ registered direct offering, with complex warrant structures tied to future FDA milestones and stock price levels, highlights both Clene’s ongoing financial strain and its need to fund operations and potential commercialization, which weighs on sentiment despite the clinical momentum. Overall, analyst and AI-based views cited in the articles reflect a tension between the company’s weak financial profile and bearish technicals versus the potentially transformative impact of favorable FDA engagement and biomarker‑driven ALS data on CNM‑Au8’s regulatory and commercial outlook, which is the key rationale behind recent price target and rating adjustments rather than any short‑term trading move.
